mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is persistently near the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL inflation is stable, equity discount rates stay suppressed and the S&P 500 should drift higher over the next month as cash flows are valued at lower yields.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of zero-rate observations, or if the signal loses directional accuracy in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend marks deteriorating household income and weaker demand, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month after the unemployment rate crosses higher by a meaningful amount.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered events produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average return after the trigger is not below unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS is accelerating while HOUST remains weak, the pattern implies consumption is holding up but housing is not transmitting a broad recovery, which should support only short-lived equity gains over the next week rather than a sustained advance.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth turns positive while HOUST stays below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform baseline over 5-day horizons, or if either single proxy alone explains the entire effect without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and earnings momentum, so the S&P 500 should post positive returns over the next quarter as production data confirm a real-economy expansion.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO changes fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign reverses during multiple rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A collapse in HOUST signals a housing-led demand slowdown that feeds into weaker consumer spending and lower equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if SPX returns are not negative in triggered housing downturn observations, or if the conditional mean return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS stops falling and UNRATE is still elevated, the market reads labor stabilization as evidence that recession risk is fading, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change turns positive while UNRATE remains above 9%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-stabilization condition does not beat unconditional SPX returns, or if PAYEMS and UNRATE effects remain additive rather than interaction-dependent.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, any upward break in FEDFUNDS above a low baseline should compress equity multiples and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next week, especially if inflation is not falling at the same time.
failure_condition: Reject if tightening triggers are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than negative ones, or if the effect disappears once CPIAUCSL is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad final-demand acceleration, which should lift corporate revenues and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with joint GDP and retail acceleration do not outperform baseline over 60-day horizons, or if either series alone accounts for the predictive content.
